nativeRange.habitat: Deeper reef slopes and walls, typically 15–25 m depth in low-light, slow-flow environments

diet.notes: Primarily photosynthetic via zooxanthellae. Benefits from target feeding 2–3 times per week with meaty marine foods: enriched mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, nauplii larvae, cyclops, finely chopped shellfish, and LPS-specific pellets. Target-feed each polyp head individually. Also absorbs dissolved organics from the water column.

avoidWith: aggressive LPS with long sweeper tentacles
large aggressive fish that may nip polyps

summary: A beginner-friendly LPS coral from Australia's deeper reefs; branching green colonies expand dramatically under moderate light with regular target feeding.
careNotes: Duncanopsammia axifuga (Duncan Coral) is a fast-growing, branching LPS coral from Australia and the South China Sea, found naturally at 15–25 m depth in calm, low-light conditions. Each polyp head is ~2.5 cm across with flowing tentacles. Colonies are fast-growing and will branch prolifically with good husbandry, requiring ample space to avoid crowding neighboring corals. Place toward the bottom of the tank in indirect, moderate flow — polyps should wave gently without being blown flat. Avoid direct high flow. Requires regular calcium and alkalinity dosing for skeletal growth. Cross-source note: temperature range varies across sources (72–78°F per LiveAquaria/Fish Laboratory vs. 76–80°F per Bulk Reef Supply); the conservative range 22–27°C is used here. Lighting PAR also varies (100–150 PAR per Fish Laboratory vs. 150–250 PAR per Bulk Reef Supply); 100–250 PAR encompasses both. Care level reported as 'beginner' by most hobbyist sources but 'moderate' by BRS and LiveAquaria; 'intermediate' is used conservatively. Calcium requirements vary slightly across sources (380–460 ppm combined range). Lifespan estimated as decades to over a century in the wild; captive specimens reliably kept for multiple decades.
breedingNotes: Readily propagated by fragging individual polyp stalks. Each established head produces approximately one new polyp per month under good conditions. Fragments heal quickly and attach to live rock. Sexual reproduction in the wild; captive sexual breeding not commonly reported.

primary.name: World Wide Corals
primary.notes: Primary source page is a generic LPS collection listing; no Duncan-specific data available. Generic LPS guidance (medium-low light, medium flow) is consistent with species-specific sources.

additional.name: Bulk Reef Supply
additional.notes: Cross-checked water parameters, lighting, and care level. BRS reports 76–80°F and 150–250 PAR; conservative ranges used to reconcile with other sources.

additional.name: Fish Laboratory
additional.notes: Detailed care guide; reports 72–78°F, 100–150 PAR, 1.023–1.025 SG, calcium 400–450 ppm, magnesium 1250–1350 ppm. Care level listed as beginner.

additional.name: LiveAquaria
additional.notes: Confirms family Dendrophylliidae, moderate care level, peaceful temperament, low-to-moderate lighting, bottom-to-middle placement, low flow, kH 8–12, SG 1.023–1.025.

additional.name: World Register of Marine Species / Animalia.bio
additional.notes: Taxonomy and native range confirmation: Order Scleractinia, Family Dendrophylliidae; range includes Australia, South China Sea, Indonesia, Papua New Guinea, Philippines, Vietnam, Taiwan.
